mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production signals rising aggregate supply and corporate revenue expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during months when INDPRO increases, or the Pearson correlation between INDPRO changes and 20-day returns is negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.5 percent signals exceptionally accommodative monetary policy that suppresses short-term discount rates and risk premia, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50 percent of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5 percent, or the average return under this threshold does not exceed the unconditional average (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously falling unemployment and rising retail sales signal robust consumer demand supported by labor income growth, jointly driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days beyond the independent effects of either series alone.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE falls and RSAFS rises simultaneously, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than periods with only one condition met (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction term coefficient is not positive in a bivariate regression over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion with unemployment below 4 percent, a rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market momentum and impending earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises more than 0.2 percentage points during periods of sub-4% unemployment, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances, or the average return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in retail sales signal contracting consumer demand and negative operating leverage for corporations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: After two consecutive monthly declines in RSAFS, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances, or the average return is not significantly negative versus the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously stagnant real GDP and rising nonfarm payrolls signal labor hoarding and negative productivity shocks that compress profit margins, jointly driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days beyond the independent effects of each series.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 is flat or falling while PAYEMS rises, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower than when both move in the same direction (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction term in a 2-factor regression carries a positive coefficient over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 285 signal entrenched inflation that reduces real earnings expectations and compresses valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL exceeds 285, S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances, or the average return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts signals durable goods demand and construction sector earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following increases in HOUST are negative in more than 50 percent of months, or the rank correlation between HOUST changes and 20-day returns falls below zero over a 3-year rolling window.
